column1: traitBxd_18069, traitBxd_18070
column2: For fear acquisition, we identified a significant locus on chromosome (Chr) 10 and eight suggestive loci on Chr 2, 4, 5, 11, 13, and 15. For fear expression, we identified one significant and another highly suggestive locus on Chr 13, as well as four suggestive loci on Chr 10, 11, and X.

column2: Major QTLs were discovered near the centromere of chromosome 5 (Eye1: genomewide P < 0.005) and on proximal chromosome 17 near the mast cell protease 6 gene (Eye2, P < 0.05). Both QTLs have significant effects on eye size, lens weight, and retinal area.

column1: traitBxd_10375, traitBxd_10376, traitBxd_10377, traitBxd_10378, traitBxd_10379, traitBxd_13031
column2: Two quantitative trait loci mapped to chromosome 1 and to chromosome 5. They individually modulate hippocampal weight and neuron number in the dentate gyrus.

column1: traitBxd_10456, traitBxd_10457, traitBxd_10458, traitBxd_10459, traitBxd_10460, traitBxd_10461, traitBxd_10462
column2: QTLs that have pronounced effects on volumes of different regions of the mouse hippocampus were mapped to Chr 13 (hippocampus proper and dentate gyrus), Chr 11 (an epistatic interaction effect on the dentate gyrus), Chr 15 (largely hippocampus proper), Chr 6 (granule cell layer of the dentate gyrus) and Chr 1 (pyramidal cell layer, with smaller effects on all regions). These loci are related to differences in the volumes of the hippocampal regions after statistically controlling for background variation in brain size.

column2: Many reports of pleiotropy. For example, two independently measured muscle weights, tibialis anterior (TA) and extensor digitorum longus (EDL), were both associated with rs27338905 on chromosome 2. Likewise, the weight of three muscles (gastrocnemius, EDL and soleus) mapped to the proximal end of chromosome 13; in each case, the minor allele was associated with increased muscle weight. Finally, on chromosome 12, pleiotropic effects on tibia length and EDL weight were identified.

column1: traitBxd_10372, traitBxd_10373, traitBxd_10374
column2: The resistance to TNF links to loci coding for corticosteroid-binding globulin (Cbg), alpha1-protease inhibitor (Spi1), contrapsin (Spi2) and the contrapsin-regulating gene Spi2r that form a gene cluster on chromosome 12. Quantitative trait-loci analysis of TNF-induced induction of IL-6 and of hypothermia also points to the importance of this locus, more particularly the Cbg and Spi2 loci, in the resistance to TNF.

column1: traitBxd_10112, traitBxd_10119, traitBxh_10015
column2: Two QTLs, mapping to exactly the same intervals on chromosomes 7 and 11, were identified that were associated with variation of both cell cycling and life span.

column1: traitBxd_10045, traitBxd_10046, traitBxd_10047, traitBxd_10048, traitBxd_10049, traitBxd_10050, traitBxd_10051, traitBxd_10052, traitBxd_10053, traitBxd_10054
column2: A QTL on Chromosome (Chr) 9 was mapped for hypothermic sensitivity to quinpirole, and a suggestive QTL in the same chromosomal region for tolerance to quinpirole after repeated treatments. Suggestive QTLs were also identified on Chr 19 for sensitivity and tolerance to quinpirole-induced hypothermia and for baseline locomotor activity; on Chr 15 for locomotor sensitivity to quinpirole; and on Chr 13 and 5 for baseline locomotor activity. Results indicate that genetic differences in quinpirole sensitivity and tolerance are associated with QTLs near Drd2, and that baseline locomotor activity is associated with a suggestive QTL in proximity to the dopamine transporter gene Dat1. These data suggest that the genes influencing locomotor activity, dopamine mimetic sensitivity, and tolerance do not overlap completely.

column1: traitBxd_10001, traitBxd_10002, traitBxd_10003, traitBxd_10004, traitBxd_10005, traitBxd_10006, traitBxd_10007
column2: Five quantitative trait loci (QTLs) that significantly control variation in cerebellar size were mapped to chromosomes 1 (Cbs1a), 8 (Cbs8a), 14 (Cbs14a), and 19 (Cbs19a, Cbs19b). The QTL Cbs8a is significantly linked to variation in IGL volume and is suggestively linked to variation in the number of cerebellar folia.

column1: traitBxd_10175, traitBxd_10176, traitBxd_10177, traitBxd_10178
column2: A QTL associated with beta values for both doses was definitively mapped to the proximal region of chromosome 17. Seven to 12 chromosomal regions were provisionally identified for each phenotype; several were associated with 2 or more phenotypes. Each QTL suggests the location of a gene or genes affecting EtOH pharmacokinetics.

column1: traitBxd_10347, traitBxd_10348, traitBxd_10349, traitBxd_10350, traitBxd_10351, traitBxd_10352, traitBxd_10353, traitBxd_10354
column2: We identified 6 provisional QTLs for initial sensitivity on four chromosomes, 6 provisional QTLs for AFT on four chromosomes, and 11 provisional QTLs for cAMP signaling on nine chromosomes. Two loci were found to overlap for measures of initial sensitivity and for cAMP signaling.

column1: traitBxd_10739, traitBxd_10740, traitBxd_10741, traitBxd_10742, traitBxd_10743, traitBxd_10744, traitBxd_10745, traitBxd_10746
column2: Quantitative trait loci (QTL) affecting muscle weight were mapped in an F2 intercross of B6 and D2 mice (B6D2F2) and BXD RIs. A total of 10 autosomal, muscle-specific, but not muscle-type-specific, QTL, explaining a total of 5.4, 7.7, 22.9, and 8.6% of phenotypic variance for soleus, TA, EDL, and gastrocnemius muscles, respectively, were found across chromosomes 1 (Chr 1), 2, 3 (female-specific), 5 (two), 6, 7, 8, and 9 in B6D2F2 mice. The QTL on Chr 8 for EDL and the female-specific QTL on Chr 3 for gastrocnemius muscles were statistically significant, but the remaining QTL were at the suggestive level of statistical significance. Ten QTL on Chr 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 14, 17 (two), and 19 were identified in BXD RIs. Half of the QTL in BXD RIs had pleiotropic effects and were at the suggestive level of significance (except for the significant QTL for gastrocnemius muscle on Chr 17).

column1: traitBxd_10656, traitBxd_10657, traitBxd_10658, traitBxd_10659, traitBxd_10660, traitBxd_10661, traitBxd_10662, traitBxd_10663, traitBxd_10664, traitBxd_10665, traitBxd_10666
column2: Quantitative trait loci regulating LacZ on day 30 were found on chromosome (Chr) 19 (33 cM) and Chr 15 (42.8 cM). Cytotoxicity mapped to Chr 7 (41.0 and 57.4-65.2 cM), Chr 15 (61.7 cM), and Chr X (27.8 cM). IFN-gamma production mapped to Chr 18 (22, 27, and 32 cM) and Chr 11 (64.0 cM). TNF-alpha and IL-6 production mapped to Chr 6 (91.5 cM) Chr 9 (42.0 cM) and Chr 8 (52 and 73.0 cM).

column1: traitBxd_10211, traitBxd_10212, traitBxd_10213, traitBxd_10214, traitBxd_10215, traitBxd_10216, traitBxd_10217, traitBxd_10218, traitBxd_10219
column2: we performed quantitative trait analysis in aged BXD recombinant inbred (RI) mice for the number and frequency of LSK cells, and for their proliferative capacity in vitro. Several novel QTL were identified. Furthermore, 4 of 7 lifespan QTL overlap with QTL contributing to the number, frequency, or proliferative capacity of LSK cells in young or old mice. Taken together, these data establish a close genetic, and perhaps functional, link between genetic variation in lifespan and characteristics of stem and progenitor cells.

column1: traitBxd_10061, traitBxd_10062
column2: A QTL affecting pulmonary TNF-α production was identified on chromosome 2, and a region affecting both polymorphonuclear leukocyte recruitment and TNF-α levels was identified on chromosome 11.

column1: traitBxd_10758, traitBxd_10759, traitBxd_10760, traitBxd_10761, traitBxd_10762, traitBxd_10763, traitBxd_10764, traitBxd_10765
column2: Linkage analyses in B6D2F2 population identified QTL affecting muscle weight on Chr 1, 2, 6, and 9. Pleiotropic influences were observed for QTL on Chr 1 (soleus, TA), 2 (TA, EDL, gastrocnemius), and 9 (soleus, TA, EDL) and were not related to muscle type (fast/slow-twitch) or function (flexor/extensor). Effect of QTL on Chr 9 on soleus muscle was male specific.

column1: traitBxd_10786, traitBxd_10787, traitBxd_10788, traitBxd_10789
column2: Marker regression and interval mapping revealed associations with quantitative trait loci (QTL) for CRF transcript abundance on chromosome 1 (at 89.2 cM), chromosome 12 (between 54-58 cM), and chromosome 13 (between 26-30 cM); for Crf-R1 transcript abundance on chromosome 7 (at 1.5 cM), chromosome 12 (at 37 cM), and chromosome X (at 30 cM); for CRF binding protein transcript abundance on chromosome 7 (at 48.5 cM), chromosome 8 (at 65 cM), and chromosome 12 (at 19 cM); and for AVP transcript abundance on chromosome 7 (at 1 cM), chromosome 12 (at 13 cM), and chromosome 13 (at 45 cM).

column2: QTL analysis revealed a highly significant QTL on distal chromosome 1 for zinc in the PFC of females. The same QTL was suggestive for zinc in the VMB of females. Other significant QTL included a region on chromosome 8 for zinc in the NA of females, and chromosome 16 for copper in the CP of males. Suggestive QTL included a QTL on chromosome 12 for copper in the CP and NA of females and a QTL on chromosome 3, 13 and 17 for copper and zinc in the PFC of males. Of particular interest, the QTL on chromosome 17 was co-incidental for copper and zinc in the PFC of males.

column1: traitBxd_11027, traitBxd_11028, traitBxd_11029, traitBxd_11030, traitBxd_11031, traitBxd_11032
column2: We identified two QTL, on chromosomes 14 and 9, most strongly associated with Cu but also suggestively associated with Fe (chr. 14) and Zn (chr. 9). We also performed genetic correlational analyses with existing data on these strains and revealed associations with cognitive, anxiety-related, and alcohol-related phenotypes.

column2: When we carried out quantitative trait locus mapping for these traits, we found that the map for IMF volume showed little overlap with the maps for the other parts of the projection or for adult neurogenesis, suggesting that to a large degree the IMF is regulated independently. The highest overlapping peak in the genome-wide association maps for IMF volume and the number of new neurons was on distal chromosome 5 (118.3-199.2 Mb) with an LRS score of 5.5 for IMF and 6.0 for new neurons.

column2: A significant QTL for acute functional tolerance in the alcohol pretreatment group was mapped to distal chromosome 4; numerous suggestive QTLs were also mapped. Preference drinking and drinking in the dark have previously been mapped to the chromosome 4 locus. The chromosome 4 QTL is a key region containing possibly pleiotropic genes for acute functional tolerance and drinking behavior.
